hook.text: A river divides a city. One side bans smoking in bars, the other doesn't. Nature just ran your experiment.

buildup.text: Natural experiments exploit real-world events that create treatment and control groups without researcher intervention.

discovery.text: The 2021 Nobel Prize in Economics went to researchers who used natural experiments to study minimum wage effects.

twist.text: Natural experiments aren't truly random. People on each side of the river may differ in ways you don't measure.

climax.text: They fill the gap where randomized experiments are impossible — policy, economics, and public health rely on them.

punchline.text: When you can't create the experiment, find one that nature already ran.

quiz.question: What is a natural experiment?
options: A study that exploits real-world events creating treatment and control groups without researcher intervention
A lab experiment using natural materials
